event: The Celestial Pilot Arrives at the Shore of Purgatory
symbols: angel with outstretched wings — the celestial pilot who ferries redeemed souls from the mouth of the Tiber to the shore of Purgatory, a figure of divine guidance
group of robed souls behind the angel — the penitent dead who died in grace, now beginning their purification
Dante and Virgil as observers at left — the pilgrim-poet witnessing the economy of salvation, reason (Virgil) and faith (Dante) beholding grace
the shore and water — the liminal boundary between the realm of the dead and the mountain of purification
luminous clouds above — the glory of heaven toward which the purgatorial journey ascends
description: A black-and-white engraving showing a shoreline scene. At the left foreground, two figures stand observing: one in a laurel wreath and the other in robes. Before them, a tall angel with large spread wings stands at the water's edge, one arm extended in a gesture of arrival or command. Behind the angel, a dense group of robed figures huddle together, having just arrived. The sky above is filled with dramatic clouds rendered in fine cross-hatching, with light breaking through from above. The water stretches out to the right. Text at the bottom identifies the scene as from Purgatorio, Canto II.
